rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH BEST VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ajax Tocco Magnethermic Corp.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 29, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Ajax Tocco Magnethermic Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant has not satisfied its burden of production on the continuation of business, fraud and product line exceptions to the general rule of no liability of an asset purchaser. . Plaintiffs¿ objections to paragraphs 2 and 6, the second sentence of paragraph 3, the third sentence of paragraph 4, the last sentence of paragraph 5 and all but the second sentence of paragraph 7 of Ms. Kold¿s 2010 affidavit are sustained. The court will consider plaintiffs¿ ex parte application for discovery at the same time as the hearing. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE MCMILLIAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sherwin-Williams Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 29, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant The Sherwin-Williams Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. By not presenting responses to Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. The 9/21/10 order regarding application of Texas law neither states nor suggests that Texas summary judgment rules apply. (See 3 Witkin, California Procedure (5th ed. 2008 and 2010 supp.) 117 (¿The basic rule is well settled that matters of procedure are governed by the law of the forum.¿)). By not presenting responses to contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot satisfy the factually-devoid prong of Aguilar. Evidentiary materials submitted with reply papers were not considered. Defendant is granted leave to file another motion for summary judgment, if it wishes to do so, that includes responses to contention discovery with its moving papers. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY ELLIOTT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, October 29, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant FMC on behalf of its Peerless Pump business¿ motion for summary judgment was continued from October 6, 2010 to give plaintiffs an opportunity to file further opposition papers. Plaintiffs did not do so. The tentative ruling remains the same: Defendant FMC Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment on behalf of its former Peerless Pump business is treated as a motion for summary adjudication by defendant that it has no liability with regard to its former Peerless Pump business and, as so treated, the motion is granted. All of plaintiffs¿ procedural objections lack merit. Plaintiffs understood and were not prejudiced by the manner in which the motion was brought. The court exercises its discretion to look beyond the separate statement to consider plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s contention interrogatories. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable fact that Mr. Elliott was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant¿s former Peerless Pump business. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HARRY ELLIOTT et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

All of plaintiffs¿ procedural objections lack merit. Plaintiffs understood and were not prejudiced by the manner in which the motion was brought. The court exercises its discretion to look beyond the separate statement to consider plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s contention interrogatories. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any evidence creating a triable fact that Mr. Elliott was exposed to any asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant¿s former Chicago Pump business. = (220/HEK)
